keyEvents.event: Battle of Britain and standing alone against Hitler
keyEvents.impact: Finest-hour mythos; self-image as defender of freedom punching above its weight

keyEvents.event: Beginning of decolonization with Indian independence
keyEvents.impact: Managed imperial retreat; influence repurposed through the Commonwealth and diplomacy
Hard lesson that the UK cannot act without Washington; the 'special relationship' becomes doctrine
Demonstrated will and expeditionary ability to defend overseas territories
Public scarred by intelligence failures; parliamentary skepticism of foreign interventions
Departure from the EU; 'Global Britain' rebrand and tilt to the Indo-Pacific (AUKUS)
Reasserted role as Europe's most forward-leaning power against Russian aggression

history.narrative: A former imperial power that converts world-class diplomacy, intelligence, and finance into outsized influence, almost always operating shoulder-to-shoulder with Washington. Post-Brexit, it compensates for European estrangement with Atlanticism, AUKUS, and a hawkish line on Russia.

redLines.description: Armed attack on a NATO member (Article 5)
Nuclear use or blackmail against the UK or NATO allies
Disruption of the City of London's role in global finance

goals.description: Preserve the special relationship and remain America's indispensable ally
Lead European resistance to Russian aggression and hybrid warfare
Project global influence post-Brexit via AUKUS, the Commonwealth, and the UN Security Council
Secure trade deals and defend the City of London as a global financial hub
Hold the Union together against Scottish secession and defend overseas territories
